content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She has a bad ___ and keeps sneezing all day.
distractor_rationale: a diet does not cause sneezing — it refers to eating habits, not an illness
a bone cannot be 'bad' in a way that causes sneezing — bones are body structures, not illnesses
breath refers to air from the lungs, not an illness that causes sneezing

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom broke his ___ when he fell off his bicycle, and the doctor put it in a cast.
distractor_rationale: a chest cannot be put in a cast — casts are used for limbs, not the torso
blood is a liquid and cannot be broken or put in a cast
the brain is inside the skull and cannot be broken and placed in a cast

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She went to the ___ because one of her teeth was hurting badly.
distractor_rationale: a diet is an eating plan, not a person you visit for tooth pain
a disease is a medical condition, not a professional you go to see
breath is air from the lungs, not a person who treats teeth

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After running up the stairs, he was out of ___ and had to stop to rest.
distractor_rationale: 'out of blood' is not a fixed expression and makes no sense after physical effort
'out of body' describes a strange sensation, not the result of running upstairs
'out of bone' is not a real expression and is meaningless in this context

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor checked her ___ pressure and said it was a little too high.
distractor_rationale: 'chest pressure' is not the medical term for this measurement — blood pressure is the fixed collocation
'bone pressure' is not a medical measurement and makes no sense here
'body pressure' is not a recognised medical term for this type of health check

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor told him to follow a healthy ___ with less sugar and more vegetables to lose weight.
distractor_rationale: a breath cannot be 'followed' and does not involve eating less sugar or more vegetables
a disease is an illness, not an eating plan you follow to lose weight
death cannot be followed as a plan for losing weight — it is the end of life

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She twisted her ___ while walking on the uneven path and could not put any weight on it.
distractor_rationale: the chest cannot be twisted while walking on a path and cannot bear weight
the brain is inside the skull and cannot be twisted by walking on an uneven surface
a diet is an eating plan and is not a body part that can be twisted or bear weight

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Doctors have found that regular exercise helps the ___ stay sharp and reduces the risk of memory loss in older adults.
distractor_rationale: an ankle controls movement of the foot and is not associated with staying sharp or memory loss
the chest contains the heart and lungs and is not linked to staying sharp or memory loss
bones provide structure to the body but are not responsible for memory or mental sharpness

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Malaria is a serious ___ that is spread by mosquitoes and can be fatal if it is not treated quickly.
distractor_rationale: a diet is an eating plan and cannot be spread by mosquitoes or be fatal
breath is air exhaled from the lungs and cannot be spread by mosquitoes
death is the result of an untreated illness, not the illness itself that mosquitoes spread

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The nurse asked him to ___ slowly and deeply so that she could listen to his lungs clearly with her stethoscope.
distractor_rationale: swallowing is a throat action and is not what a stethoscope is used to monitor for lung examination
coughing is an involuntary reflex and cannot be done 'slowly and deeply' to help listen to the lungs
blinking is a movement of the eyelids and is completely unrelated to lung examination with a stethoscope

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom went to the ___ because he had a bad toothache and needed a filling.
distractor_rationale: a doctor treats general medical conditions, not teeth or fillings
a nurse assists with medical care but does not perform dental fillings
a chemist sells medicine but does not treat toothaches or do fillings

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She twisted her ___ when she stepped off the kerb and had to sit down immediately because she could not walk on it.
distractor_rationale: an elbow is on the arm and is not involved in stepping or walking
a shoulder cannot be twisted by stepping off a kerb and does not affect walking
a wrist is on the hand and twisting it would not prevent someone from walking

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor told Maria to improve her ___ by eating more vegetables and cutting down on fatty foods every day.
distractor_rationale: breath cannot be improved by eating vegetables and cutting down on fatty foods in this medical context
blood is not something you improve by changing what you eat in this general sense — the collocation 'improve your blood' does not exist
bone cannot be improved as a single uncountable noun by eating more vegetables and cutting fatty foods in this standard medical instruction

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James had a very bad ___ last winter and spent three days in bed with a runny nose, a sore throat, and a high temperature.
distractor_rationale: disease refers to a serious long-term medical condition, not a short illness with a runny nose and sore throat lasting three days
death is the end of life and cannot be experienced for three days with a runny nose and sore throat
diet refers to eating habits and has no connection to a runny nose, sore throat, or high temperature

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After running up four flights of stairs, Anna stopped at the top and tried to slow her ___ down by inhaling deeply through her nose and exhaling slowly through her mouth.
distractor_rationale: chest is a body part and cannot be slowed down by inhaling and exhaling — you slow your breathing, not your chest
brain is an organ and is not slowed down by inhaling and exhaling after physical exercise
blood cannot be slowed down by inhaling through the nose and exhaling through the mouth in this direct sense — the collocation 'slow your blood down' by breathing is not standard

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She twisted her ___ when she stepped off the pavement and fell to the ground.
distractor_rationale: the elbow is part of the arm and cannot be twisted by stepping off a pavement
the shoulder is too high up on the body to be injured by stepping off a pavement
the wrist is part of the hand and arm, not the foot, so stepping off a pavement would not twist it

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom had a bad ___ all week — he kept sneezing and his nose was running constantly.
distractor_rationale: a diet is an eating plan, not an illness with sneezing and a runny nose
a disease is a serious long-term condition, whereas sneezing and a runny nose for one week describe a common cold
an injury is physical damage from an accident, not a condition causing sneezing and a runny nose

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor said the X-ray showed that the ___ in her leg was broken and she needed a cast.
distractor_rationale: blood cannot be seen as broken on an X-ray and does not require a cast
breath is air from the lungs and is invisible on an X-ray
skin is not visible on an X-ray and cannot be described as broken in a way that requires a cast

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The nurse pressed a cloth firmly against the cut on his arm to stop the ___ from flowing out.
distractor_rationale: breath is air and does not flow from a cut on the arm
bone does not flow from a cut and cannot be stopped with a cloth
sweat does not flow from a cut and is not stopped by pressing a cloth against a wound

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria visited the ___ because one of her teeth had been hurting for several days and needed to be checked.
distractor_rationale: a doctor treats general health problems but does not examine or treat teeth
a pharmacist sells medicines but does not examine or treat teeth
a surgeon performs operations on the body but is not the first professional you visit for a painful tooth

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After running up four flights of stairs, James was out of ___ and had to stop to rest for a moment.
distractor_rationale: you cannot be 'out of blood' — this is not a fixed collocation and has no meaning in this context
'out of energy' is not a standard fixed collocation in English the way 'out of breath' is, and energy does not relate specifically to the physical act of breathing after running
'out of strength' is not a fixed collocation in English and does not describe the specific breathing difficulty caused by running upstairs

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor advised her to improve her ___ by eating more vegetables and cutting down on fatty food.
distractor_rationale: a disease is an illness, not something you improve by choosing what to eat
a treatment is medical care given for an illness, not something improved by changing what you eat
exercise refers to physical activity, not to the food choices described in the sentence

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Scientists believe that regular physical activity helps the ___ stay sharp by increasing blood flow and forming new connections between nerve cells.
distractor_rationale: the chest is a physical cavity containing the lungs and heart and does not form connections between nerve cells or stay sharp
bone is hard tissue that does not form nerve connections or become sharp through physical activity
while the whole body benefits from exercise, only the brain forms nerve connections and stays cognitively sharp, so 'body' is too vague and contradicts the specific mechanism described

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The charity works to raise awareness of a rare ___ that affects the immune system and has no known cure at present.
distractor_rationale: a diet is an eating pattern, not a condition that affects the immune system or requires a cure
a cold is a mild, short-term viral illness with a known recovery process, not a rare condition with no cure that affects the immune system
an injury is physical damage caused by an accident, not a condition affecting the immune system that requires a cure

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The patient felt a sharp pain in her ___ and found it difficult to take deep breaths, so the doctor ordered a scan of her lungs.
distractor_rationale: pain in the ankle would not cause difficulty breathing or require a lung scan
pain in the arm would not cause difficulty taking deep breaths or lead a doctor to scan the lungs
the brain does not contain the lungs, and brain pain would not cause breathing difficulty or require a lung scan

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor said the cut was not ___. (DANGER)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She felt very ___ after running up the stairs. (BREATH)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The nurse checked the patient's ___ pressure twice during the morning visit. (BLOOD)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The doctor explained that a healthy ___ with plenty of vegetables can help prevent many diseases. (DIET)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the patient came as a shock to the whole family, as he had seemed to be recovering well. (DEAD)
